text:
Text detection in natural scene images is a critical prerequisite for many content-based image analysis tasks. In this paper, we propose an accurate and robust method for scene text detection that leverages the strengths of Maximally Stable Extremal Regions (MSERs). We first design a fast and effective pruning algorithm to extract MSERs as character candidates by minimizing regularized variations. These candidates are then grouped into potential text regions using a single-link clustering algorithm. To optimize this process, we introduce a novel self-training distance metric learning algorithm that automatically determines distance weights and clustering thresholds.

To refine the results, we estimate the posterior probabilities of text candidates being non-text using a specialized character classifier. Candidates with high non-text probabilities are eliminated, and the remaining regions are verified by a final text classifier. We evaluated our system on the ICDAR 2011 Robust Reading Competition database, achieving an f-measure of over 76%—a significant improvement over the previous state-of-the-art performance of 71%. Further experiments on multilingual, street view, multi-orientation, and born-digital databases demonstrate the versatility and effectiveness of the proposed approach.
